Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Asuka, Nara]] 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Asuka, Nara]] era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Tumulus === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database * Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | other_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | lat_deg = 34 | lat_min = 27 | lat_sec = 51.25 | long_deg = 135 | long_min = 48 | long_sec = 21.95 | form = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | dimensions = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial_facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designated_as = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a designated National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially designated tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal form and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal shape. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''Dimensions:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === Burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''Structure:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === Artifacts === Excavations revealed: * Tuff artifacts shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historical Site database *
