Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|12=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|12=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|12=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|12=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{draft categories| [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nakaoyama Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|12=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|12=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{draft categories| [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nakaoyama Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|12=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|12=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{draft categories| [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nakaoyama Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]]{{draft categories| {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|12=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|12=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{draft categories| [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nakaoyama Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]]{{draft categories| {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nakaoyama Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft|12=draft}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|12=draft|12=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft|12=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{draft categories| [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft|12=draft}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|12=draft|12=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft|12=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{draft categories| [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki {{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nakaoyama Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{draft categories| [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Monmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Wikipeddia drafts]] [[Category:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ja:中尾山古墳]] [[en:Nakaoyama Kofun]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{draft categories| [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 中尾山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（[[2020年]]度調査時） |別名 = 中尾塚/中尾石墓 |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]大字平田
（[[国営飛鳥歴史公園]] 高松塚古墳周辺地区内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 51.25 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 21.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[八角墳]] |規模 = 対辺長19.5m
高さ4m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]] |出土品 = 沓形石造物・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[8世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = （一説）[[文武天皇]] |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「中尾山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 八角墳 |ラベル = 中尾山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''中尾山古墳'''（なかおやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]平田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 第42代[[文武天皇]]の真陵とする説が有力視される。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]南東縁、[[文武天皇]]陵（栗原塚穴古墳）から北に延びる丘陵頂部に築造された古墳である。丘陵上には文武天皇陵のほか[[高松塚古墳]]が所在する。[[鎌倉時代]]に盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）・[[2020年]]度（[[令和]]2年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は八角形で、対辺長約19.5メートル・高さ4メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘は3段築成で、版築によって構築される{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘の1段目・2段目は基壇状の石積みとし、3段目は盛土のみとする。墳丘周囲には三重の外周石敷が巡らされる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、底石・奥壁・閉塞石・天井石各1石、側壁各2石、隅石（柱石）4石（1石は欠失）の計10石（現存9石）から構成される。石槨内面には平滑な磨きがかけられて水銀朱が塗布されるほか、石槨中央部に火葬骨の蔵骨器が納められたと見られる。副葬品は詳らかでない。 この中尾山古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[8世紀]]初頭頃の築造と推定される。天皇陵級の古墳に見られる八角墳である点、豪壮な横口式石槨を有する最高級の火葬墓（火葬墳）である点で特異な古墳になる。被葬者は明らかでないが、近年では第42代[[文武天皇]]（[[707年]]崩御）の真陵とする説が有力視される（現墓は南の栗原塚穴古墳に治定）。 古墳域は[[1927年]]（昭和2年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[鎌倉時代]]、盗掘{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 * [[元禄]]年間（[[1688年|1688]]-[[1704年]]）、奈良奉行所与力の玉井与左右衛門が石槨を実見。 * [[享保]]年間（[[1716年|1716]]-[[1736年]]）、文武天皇陵に比定する説（『大和志』・『大和名所図会』）{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。 * [[明治]]期、踏査（野淵龍潜ら、『古墳墓取調帳』）。 * [[大正]]期、墳丘測量（上田三平ら）。 * [[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）4月8日、国の[[史跡]]に指定。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、墳丘測量。八角墳の可能性浮上（藤井利章ら）。[https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html 中尾山古墳]（明日香村ホームページ）。。 * [[1974年]]（昭和49年）、史跡環境整備に伴う発掘調査。八角墳と判明、真の文武天皇陵とする説が有力視（明日香村教育委員会・橿原考古学研究所、[[1975年]]に報告書刊行）。 * [[2020年]]度（[[令和]]2年度）、発掘調査。真の文武天皇陵とする説が確実視（明日香村教育委員会・関西大学文学部考古学研究室）{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 * [[2024年]]（令和6年）2月21日、史跡範囲の追加指定。 == 構造 == [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}{{small|右手前に沓形石造物。2020年度調査時。}}]] 墳丘の規模は、対辺長約19.5メートル・高さ4メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘の1段目・2段目は基壇状の石積みとし、裾部に花崗岩の根石を並べ、その上にこぶし大-人頭大の石材を小口積みし、さらにその上に根石と同じ石材を積み上げる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘の3段目は版築の盛土のみで、八角形に整形される{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘3段目の東側には鎌倉時代の盗掘坑が残る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 墳丘周囲には外周石敷が三重に巡らされる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。外周石敷は広範で、三重目の対辺長は約32.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。また墳丘周囲では沓形石造物2個が検出されている。一重目の上面で出土した沓形石造物は火山礫凝灰岩（竜山石）製で、幅約95センチメートル・高さ約67センチメートルを測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。表面は平滑に仕上げられているほか、端面は約135度に加工されていることから、墳頂部の装飾に使用されたと見られる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 File:Nakaoyama Kofun, ishijiki.jpg|外周石敷の隅部
{{small|八角形の角を呈する。2020年度調査時。}} File:Nakaoyama Kofun, sekizoubutsu.jpg|沓形石造物
{{small|2020年度調査時。}} == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[横口式石槨]]}}{{small|2020年度調査時。}}]] [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|横口式石槨内部}}]] 埋葬施設としては、[[横口式石槨]]が構築されている。底石1石・側壁2石・奥壁1石・閉塞石1石・天井石1石・隅石（柱石）4石（閉塞石東側の1石は盗掘で欠失）の計10石（現存9石）から構成され、底石は片麻状石英閃緑岩製の直方体、側壁・奥壁・閉塞石・隅石は火山礫凝灰岩（竜山石）製の直方体、天井石は細粒黒雲母花崗岩製である{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。石槨の内法は幅約90センチメートル・奥行約90センチメートルを測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 石槨内面には非常に丁寧な磨きがかけられているほか、全面に水銀朱が塗布される{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。側壁にはL字形の加工が認められる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。底石の中央部には60センチメートル四方の範囲で深さ1センチメートルの削り込みがなされており、火葬骨を納める蔵骨器を安置する台が据えられたと見られる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。盗掘のため蔵骨器の所在は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]][[三の丸尚蔵館]]所蔵の金銅製四環壺との関連を指摘する説が挙げられている[[河上邦彦]]『飛鳥発掘物語』扶桑社、2004年、pp. 133-135。。 閉塞石の南側には墓道が設けられており、幅3.2メートル・深さ1.3メートルを測り、埋葬後には版築によって埋め戻されている{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。この墓道の下面では暗渠の排水溝が検出されており、幅約100センチメートル・深さ約20センチメートルを測り、こぶし大の川原石で充填されている{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 File:Nakaoyama Kofun, bodou.jpg|墓道埋土の版築・暗渠排水溝
{{small|2020年度調査時。}} == 被葬者 == [[File:Emperor Monmu.jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|第42代[[文武天皇]]}}{{small|[[道成寺]]蔵。}}]] [[File:Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|文武天皇治定陵
（栗原塚穴古墳）}}]] 中尾山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、第42代天皇の'''[[文武天皇]]'''に比定する説が有力視される。文武天皇は[[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]）6月15日に25歳で崩御し、陵について『[[続日本紀]]』では「檜隈安古山陵」・「安古山陵」と見え、慶雲4年（707年）10月3日に造山陵司を任じ、11月12日に遺骸を飛鳥岡で火葬、11月20日に陵に奉葬と見える{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]では遠陵の「檜前安古岡上陵」として記載され、[[大和国]][[高市郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西3町・南北3町で陵戸5烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。 その後、[[中世]]期には陵の所在に関する所伝は喪失{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。[[元禄]]10年（[[1697年]]）の[[江戸幕府]]による元禄の探陵では、奈良奉行所が高松塚古墳を文武天皇陵の未分明陵と報告したことで高松塚古墳に決定され、享保・文化の陵改めの際にも踏襲された{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。一方、『大和志』・『大和名所図会』では中尾山古墳に比定する説、『打墨縄』では[[野口王墓|野口王墓古墳]]（現在の[[天武天皇]]・[[持統天皇]]合葬陵）とする異説が挙げられている{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。その後、安政の陵改めの際には、野口王墓古墳が文武天皇陵に改定された{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。[[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）2月1日の上奏裁可を経て、御園村の古水帳に「アンコウ」と記す栗原塚穴古墳に文武天皇陵が再改定され、現在に至っている{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。 一方で中尾山古墳では、立地・築造時期のほか、埋葬主体に豪壮な横口式石槨が使用される火葬墳であり、1974年（昭和49年）の調査で八角墳と判明したことから、近年では真の文武天皇陵であるとする説が有力視されている。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+檜隈大内陵・檜隈安古岡上陵治定の変遷 ! ![[野口王墓|野口王墓古墳]] !栗原塚穴古墳 ![[丸山古墳 (橿原市)|五条野丸山古墳]] ![[高松塚古墳]] !'''中尾山古墳''' |- !元禄 |天武・持統天皇陵 | | |文武天皇陵 | |- !安政 |文武天皇陵 | |天武・持統天皇陵 | | |- !現在 |[[天武天皇|天武]]・[[持統天皇]]陵
（檜隈大内陵） |[[文武天皇]]陵
（檜隈安古岡上陵） |畝傍陵墓参考地
・国の史跡 |国の特別史跡 |国の史跡 |} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 中尾山古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1961|中尾山古墳}}、2024年（令和6年）2月21日に史跡範囲の追加指定令和6年2月21日文部科学省告示第16号。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]大字平田（[[国営飛鳥歴史公園]] 高松塚古墳周辺地区内） '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[近畿日本鉄道]]（近鉄）[[近鉄吉野線|吉野線]] [[飛鳥駅]]（徒歩約15分） '''周辺''' * [[野口王墓|野口王墓古墳]]（天武・持統天皇合葬陵） * 栗原塚穴古墳（文武天皇陵） * [[高松塚古墳]] - 国の特別史跡。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/bunkazai/imgs/25.pdf 「中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財25）」]}}|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}}} 明日香村教育委員会、2020年。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=|chapter=|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=網干善教「中尾山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=[[直木孝次郎]]「文武天皇」|ref={{Harvid|文武天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔「檜隈安古岡上陵」（文武天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=猪熊兼勝|year=|chapter=中尾山古墳|title=[[日本大百科全書]]（ニッポニカ）|publisher=[[小学館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=中尾山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=猪熊兼勝|year=1989|chapter=中尾山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%AD%E5%B0%BE%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444606 中尾山古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=中尾山古墳環境整備委員会編|author=|year=1975|chapter=|title=史跡中尾山古墳環境整備事業報告書|publisher=奈良県明日香村|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[文武天皇]] * [[今城塚古墳]] - 中尾山古墳同様、学術調査で真の継体天皇陵に比定。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nakaoyama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1961|中尾山古墳}} * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html 中尾山古墳] - 明日香村ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:なかおやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:文武天皇]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[中尾山古墳]] to [[Nakaoyama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 中尾山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（[[2020年]]度調査時） |別名 = 中尾塚/中尾石墓 |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]大字平田
（[[国営飛鳥歴史公園]] 高松塚古墳周辺地区内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 51.25 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 21.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[八角墳]] |規模 = 対辺長19.5m
高さ4m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]] |出土品 = 沓形石造物・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[8世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = （一説）[[文武天皇]] |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「中尾山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 八角墳 |ラベル = 中尾山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''中尾山古墳'''（なかおやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]平田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 第42代[[文武天皇]]の真陵とする説が有力視される。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]南東縁、[[文武天皇]]陵（栗原塚穴古墳）から北に延びる丘陵頂部に築造された古墳である。丘陵上には文武天皇陵のほか[[高松塚古墳]]が所在する。[[鎌倉時代]]に盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）・[[2020年]]度（[[令和]]2年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は八角形で、対辺長約19.5メートル・高さ4メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘は3段築成で、版築によって構築される{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘の1段目・2段目は基壇状の石積みとし、3段目は盛土のみとする。墳丘周囲には三重の外周石敷が巡らされる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、底石・奥壁・閉塞石・天井石各1石、側壁各2石、隅石（柱石）4石（1石は欠失）の計10石（現存9石）から構成される。石槨内面には平滑な磨きがかけられて水銀朱が塗布されるほか、石槨中央部に火葬骨の蔵骨器が納められたと見られる。副葬品は詳らかでない。 この中尾山古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[8世紀]]初頭頃の築造と推定される。天皇陵級の古墳に見られる八角墳である点、豪壮な横口式石槨を有する最高級の火葬墓（火葬墳）である点で特異な古墳になる。被葬者は明らかでないが、近年では第42代[[文武天皇]]（[[707年]]崩御）の真陵とする説が有力視される（現墓は南の栗原塚穴古墳に治定）。 古墳域は[[1927年]]（昭和2年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[鎌倉時代]]、盗掘{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 * [[元禄]]年間（[[1688年|1688]]-[[1704年]]）、奈良奉行所与力の玉井与左右衛門が石槨を実見。 * [[享保]]年間（[[1716年|1716]]-[[1736年]]）、文武天皇陵に比定する説（『大和志』・『大和名所図会』）{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。 * [[明治]]期、踏査（野淵龍潜ら、『古墳墓取調帳』）。 * [[大正]]期、墳丘測量（上田三平ら）。 * [[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）4月8日、国の[[史跡]]に指定。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、墳丘測量。八角墳の可能性浮上（藤井利章ら）。[https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html 中尾山古墳]（明日香村ホームページ）。。 * [[1974年]]（昭和49年）、史跡環境整備に伴う発掘調査。八角墳と判明、真の文武天皇陵とする説が有力視（明日香村教育委員会・橿原考古学研究所、[[1975年]]に報告書刊行）。 * [[2020年]]度（[[令和]]2年度）、発掘調査。真の文武天皇陵とする説が確実視（明日香村教育委員会・関西大学文学部考古学研究室）{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 * [[2024年]]（令和6年）2月21日、史跡範囲の追加指定。 == 構造 == [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}{{small|右手前に沓形石造物。2020年度調査時。}}]] 墳丘の規模は、対辺長約19.5メートル・高さ4メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘の1段目・2段目は基壇状の石積みとし、裾部に花崗岩の根石を並べ、その上にこぶし大-人頭大の石材を小口積みし、さらにその上に根石と同じ石材を積み上げる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘の3段目は版築の盛土のみで、八角形に整形される{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。墳丘3段目の東側には鎌倉時代の盗掘坑が残る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 墳丘周囲には外周石敷が三重に巡らされる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。外周石敷は広範で、三重目の対辺長は約32.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。また墳丘周囲では沓形石造物2個が検出されている。一重目の上面で出土した沓形石造物は火山礫凝灰岩（竜山石）製で、幅約95センチメートル・高さ約67センチメートルを測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。表面は平滑に仕上げられているほか、端面は約135度に加工されていることから、墳頂部の装飾に使用されたと見られる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 File:Nakaoyama Kofun, ishijiki.jpg|外周石敷の隅部
{{small|八角形の角を呈する。2020年度調査時。}} File:Nakaoyama Kofun, sekizoubutsu.jpg|沓形石造物
{{small|2020年度調査時。}} == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[横口式石槨]]}}{{small|2020年度調査時。}}]] [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|横口式石槨内部}}]] 埋葬施設としては、[[横口式石槨]]が構築されている。底石1石・側壁2石・奥壁1石・閉塞石1石・天井石1石・隅石（柱石）4石（閉塞石東側の1石は盗掘で欠失）の計10石（現存9石）から構成され、底石は片麻状石英閃緑岩製の直方体、側壁・奥壁・閉塞石・隅石は火山礫凝灰岩（竜山石）製の直方体、天井石は細粒黒雲母花崗岩製である{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。石槨の内法は幅約90センチメートル・奥行約90センチメートルを測る{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 石槨内面には非常に丁寧な磨きがかけられているほか、全面に水銀朱が塗布される{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。側壁にはL字形の加工が認められる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。底石の中央部には60センチメートル四方の範囲で深さ1センチメートルの削り込みがなされており、火葬骨を納める蔵骨器を安置する台が据えられたと見られる{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。盗掘のため蔵骨器の所在は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]][[三の丸尚蔵館]]所蔵の金銅製四環壺との関連を指摘する説が挙げられている[[河上邦彦]]『飛鳥発掘物語』扶桑社、2004年、pp. 133-135。。 閉塞石の南側には墓道が設けられており、幅3.2メートル・深さ1.3メートルを測り、埋葬後には版築によって埋め戻されている{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。この墓道の下面では暗渠の排水溝が検出されており、幅約100センチメートル・深さ約20センチメートルを測り、こぶし大の川原石で充填されている{{Sfn|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}。 File:Nakaoyama Kofun, bodou.jpg|墓道埋土の版築・暗渠排水溝
{{small|2020年度調査時。}} == 被葬者 == [[File:Emperor Monmu.jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|第42代[[文武天皇]]}}{{small|[[道成寺]]蔵。}}]] [[File:Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|文武天皇治定陵
（栗原塚穴古墳）}}]] 中尾山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、第42代天皇の'''[[文武天皇]]'''に比定する説が有力視される。文武天皇は[[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]）6月15日に25歳で崩御し、陵について『[[続日本紀]]』では「檜隈安古山陵」・「安古山陵」と見え、慶雲4年（707年）10月3日に造山陵司を任じ、11月12日に遺骸を飛鳥岡で火葬、11月20日に陵に奉葬と見える{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]では遠陵の「檜前安古岡上陵」として記載され、[[大和国]][[高市郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西3町・南北3町で陵戸5烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。 その後、[[中世]]期には陵の所在に関する所伝は喪失{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。[[元禄]]10年（[[1697年]]）の[[江戸幕府]]による元禄の探陵では、奈良奉行所が高松塚古墳を文武天皇陵の未分明陵と報告したことで高松塚古墳に決定され、享保・文化の陵改めの際にも踏襲された{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。一方、『大和志』・『大和名所図会』では中尾山古墳に比定する説、『打墨縄』では[[野口王墓|野口王墓古墳]]（現在の[[天武天皇]]・[[持統天皇]]合葬陵）とする異説が挙げられている{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。その後、安政の陵改めの際には、野口王墓古墳が文武天皇陵に改定された{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。[[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）2月1日の上奏裁可を経て、御園村の古水帳に「アンコウ」と記す栗原塚穴古墳に文武天皇陵が再改定され、現在に至っている{{Sfn|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}。 一方で中尾山古墳では、立地・築造時期のほか、埋葬主体に豪壮な横口式石槨が使用される火葬墳であり、1974年（昭和49年）の調査で八角墳と判明したことから、近年では真の文武天皇陵であるとする説が有力視されている。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+檜隈大内陵・檜隈安古岡上陵治定の変遷 ! ![[野口王墓|野口王墓古墳]] !栗原塚穴古墳 ![[丸山古墳 (橿原市)|五条野丸山古墳]] ![[高松塚古墳]] !'''中尾山古墳''' |- !元禄 |天武・持統天皇陵 | | |文武天皇陵 | |- !安政 |文武天皇陵 | |天武・持統天皇陵 | | |- !現在 |[[天武天皇|天武]]・[[持統天皇]]陵
（檜隈大内陵） |[[文武天皇]]陵
（檜隈安古岡上陵） |畝傍陵墓参考地
・国の史跡 |国の特別史跡 |国の史跡 |} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 中尾山古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1961|中尾山古墳}}、2024年（令和6年）2月21日に史跡範囲の追加指定令和6年2月21日文部科学省告示第16号。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]大字平田（[[国営飛鳥歴史公園]] 高松塚古墳周辺地区内） '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[近畿日本鉄道]]（近鉄）[[近鉄吉野線|吉野線]] [[飛鳥駅]]（徒歩約15分） '''周辺''' * [[野口王墓|野口王墓古墳]]（天武・持統天皇合葬陵） * 栗原塚穴古墳（文武天皇陵） * [[高松塚古墳]] - 国の特別史跡。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/bunkazai/imgs/25.pdf 「中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財25）」]}}|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（明日香村の文化財）|2020}}}} 明日香村教育委員会、2020年。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=|chapter=|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=網干善教「中尾山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=[[直木孝次郎]]「文武天皇」|ref={{Harvid|文武天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔「檜隈安古岡上陵」（文武天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|檜隈安古岡上陵（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=猪熊兼勝|year=|chapter=中尾山古墳|title=[[日本大百科全書]]（ニッポニカ）|publisher=[[小学館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=中尾山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=猪熊兼勝|year=1989|chapter=中尾山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%B8%AD%E5%B0%BE%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444606 中尾山古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|中尾山古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=中尾山古墳環境整備委員会編|author=|year=1975|chapter=|title=史跡中尾山古墳環境整備事業報告書|publisher=奈良県明日香村|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[文武天皇]] * [[今城塚古墳]] -
