Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama (in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|12=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|12=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|12=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|12=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|qq=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|qq=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|qq=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|qq=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|qq=draft}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|qq=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|qq=draft}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|qq=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|qq=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|qq=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|qq=draft}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|qq=draft}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|qq=draft}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaoyama Kofun|qq=}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Historical National Government Park|qq=}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8th century|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8th century|qq=}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku era|qq=}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Historical National Government Park|qq=}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Station|qq=}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaoyama Kofun|qq=}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaoyama Kofun|qq=}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Historical National Government Park|qq=}}, near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | era = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8th century|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}, Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8th century|qq=}}, reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}: Looting incident. * {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku era|qq=}}: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the {{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|ja|国営飛鳥歴史公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Historical National Government Park|qq=}} and is accessible by foot from {{ill|Asuka Station|ja|飛鳥駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Station|qq=}} (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaoyama Kofun|qq=}} on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]], near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | era = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]: Looting incident. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]]: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]] and is accessible by foot from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中尾山古墳|version=99994907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Monmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]][Category:qq]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] }} [[every:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[sn:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[ja:中尾山古墳]] [[en:Nakaoyama Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]], near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | era = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]: Looting incident. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]]: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]] and is accessible by foot from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中尾山古墳|version=99994907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]: Looting incident. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]]: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]] and is accessible by foot from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]], near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | era = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]: Looting incident. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]]: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]] and is accessible by foot from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]: Looting incident. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]]: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]] and is accessible by foot from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]], near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | era = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]: Looting incident. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]]: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]] and is accessible by foot from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中尾山古墳|version=99994907|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakaoyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakaoyama Kofun (中尾山古墳) | image = [[File:Nakaoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun during 2020 excavation | alternate_name = Nakaotsuka/Nakao Stone Tomb | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture
(in the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]], near Takamatsuzuka Kofun area) | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 27 | coordinates = 51.25 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 21.95 | type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 19.5m
Height: 4m | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber with frontal entrance|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | era = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] | designation1 = National Historic Site }} '''Nakaoyama Kofun''' (中尾山古墳) is an {{ill|octagonal kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/octagonal kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Taira, Nara Prefecture. It is a Historic Site1 National Historic Site of Japan and is believed by many to be the true tomb of the 42nd [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. == Overview == The kofun is located at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a hill extending north from the officially Historic Site1 tomb of Emperor Monmu (Kurihara Tsukaana Kofun). Other notable tumuli such as [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] are nearby. The tomb has undergone looting during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], with archaeological investigations conducted in 1974 and 2020. The tumulus is octagonal, measuring approximately 19.5 meters in side length and over 4 meters in height. It consists of three tiers constructed using rammed-earth methods. The first and second tiers feature stone embankments, while the third is composed solely of earth. The entire mound is surrounded by three concentric stone-paved perimeters. The burial chamber is a horizontal stone chamber with a frontal entrance, made of ten stones (nine extant) with polished surfaces and coated with cinnabar. The kofun is dated to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], reflecting high-status burial practices. The unique octagonal type and elaborate stone chamber make it one of the most prestigious cremation tombs of its time. == Cultural Significance == Nakaoyama Kofun is considered a monumental structure representative of the final phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It exemplifies the architectural grandeur associated with imperial burials. The tomb’s octagonal design aligns it with other imperial tombs, such as those of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. Recent studies strongly suggest it is the actual burial site of Emperor Monmu, who died in 707 AD. == Historical Record == * [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]: Looting incident. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]]: Official inspection by Tamai Yosaburō. * 1927: Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1974: Archaeological excavation confirmed the octagonal type. * 2020: Further excavations reinforced the theory of Emperor Monmu’s burial. == Key Features == === Kofun === The octagonal tumulus is constructed with a three-tiered structure: * '''area:''' Side length: 19.5m; height: 4m. * '''Materials:''' Granite and tuff stones for the embankments, with meticulous craftsmanship evident in the upper tiers. === burial Chamber === The horizontal stone chamber includes: * '''Materials:''' Rectangular blocks of tuff (Ryōzan stone) and granite. * '''burial:''' Ten stones (nine extant), with a polished interior coated in cinnabar. * '''Central recess:''' A square depression for a reliquary, presumably for cremated remains. === burial goods === Excavations revealed: * Tuff burial goods shaped like shoes, possibly decorative components. * Remains of cinnabar-coated burial accessories. == Accessibility == The site is located near the [[:en:Asuka Historical National Government Park|Asuka Historical National Government Park]] and is accessible by foot from [[:en:Asuka Station|Asuka Station]] (approximately 15 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] on the Asuka Historic Site database * [https://asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/nakao/nakao.html Nakaoyama Kofun Official Site] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated
